commands.repair_reason: The remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof plus maintainer security review, not an automated code repair.; Cleared: The diff narrows gateway method dispatch to the accepting server context and does not add dependencies, credential handling, broader permissions, or new external execution paths. (sha=4042d38f04ad5e0439f2b093b6714ed7350a7e27)
commands.review_summary:
The branch injects a server-local plugin route registry and GatewayRequestContext into plugin HTTP request/upgrade scopes, adds regression coverage, and updates the changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main creates plugin HTTP scopes without a gateway context and dispatch then falls back to the process-global context. I did not establish a live two-gateway runtime repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof plus maintainer security review, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix: Admin HTTP RPC can execute against another live gateway instance
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: plugin HTTP routes could call `dispatchGatewayMethod()` with a scoped client but no server-local gateway context, so `admin-http-rpc` could fall back to the process-global gateway context when multiple gateway instances lived in one Node process.
- What changed: plugin HTTP route dispatch now resolves routes from the accepting server's current plugin registry and injects that same server's `GatewayRequestContext` into request/upgrade runtime scopes.
- Follow-up hardening: dynamic plugin HTTP route registration now also runs under the accepting server's route-registry scope for channel startup, plugin services, and gateway-start hooks.
- Guardrail: added regression tests for cross-gateway route/context dispatch, scoped dynamic route registration, and source-delivery target matching without the old slow normalization path.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Security hardening

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83486

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: admin HTTP RPC/plugin HTTP control-plane dispatch and dynamic plugin HTTP route registration are bound to the gateway instance that accepted/started them, preventing cross-gateway in-process dispatch when multiple gateway servers are live.

Real environment tested: macOS source checkout for focused gateway/plugin HTTP regression tests plus OpenClaw Blacksmith Testbox for `pnpm check:changed` on Linux CI image.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/http-registry.test.ts src/plugins/services.test.ts src/infra/outbound/source-delivery-plan.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.named-agent.test.ts src/gateway/server/plugins-http.runtime-scopes.test.ts src/gateway/server/plugins-http.test.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-state.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/gateway-method-runtime.test.ts extensions/admin-http-rpc/src/handler.test.ts --run
codex review --base origin/main
pnpm check:changed
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ... --run
Test Files 13 passed (13)
Tests 142 passed (142)

codex review --base origin/main
No discrete correctness issues found in the diff.

pnpm check:changed
blacksmith-testbox leaseId=tbx_01krxrzxefxewcwhvvcm93as4y exitCode=0
GitHub Actions run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26041008893
```

Observed result after fix: server A dispatches server A's plugin HTTP route with server A's gateway context even while another registry is active/pinned, dynamic route registration lands in the scoped server registry, and the changed gate passes on the rebased head.

What was not tested: a manual packaged two-gateway admin HTTP RPC session with live credentials; the in-process route/context mismatch is covered by focused gateway runtime tests and the Testbox changed gate.

## Root Cause

`dispatchGatewayMethodInProcessRaw()` correctly prefers `scope?.context`, but plugin HTTP routes only populated the request scope with a synthetic client. When no scoped context was present, dispatch fell back to the single process-global gateway context resolver. In a multi-gateway process, that resolver could point at the last-started gateway, so a request accepted by gateway A could execute against gateway B. The first PR shape added a WeakMap by registry, but that still failed when route registries were globally pinned or shared.

## Fix

- Removed the registry WeakMap context plumbing.
- Added server-local route registry and gateway context callbacks to the plugin HTTP request/upgrade handlers.
- Passed the current mutable server plugin registry and gateway request context from `startGatewayServer()` into `createGatewayRuntimeState()`.
- Used the same server-local registry resolver for route auth, node capability routes, and cached plugin HTTP handlers.
- Added scoped route registration for channel startup, plugin services, and gateway-start hooks.
- Added changelog coverage for the user-visible security hardening.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes, in-process gateway method dispatch is now bound to the correct accepting server instance.
- Data access scope changed? Yes, the fix prevents an authenticated caller on one gateway from dispatching into another live gateway instance in the same process.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: Yes.
- Config/env changes: No.
- Migration needed: No.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: plugin HTTP route reload behavior could use stale routes.
- Mitigation: the handler receives a live registry resolver over the mutable server-local `pluginRegistry`; regression tests cover stale explicit registry, active/pinned registry mismatch, scoped route registration, and current server context dispatch.
target.author: coygeek
